hooks.prompt: Review this file edit for security vulnerabilities. Flag these DEFINITE issues: 1) hardcoded secrets/API keys (NOT project IDs, resource names, or non-secret identifiers like GCP project names), 2) SQL injection, XSS, command injection, 3) authentication bypass, insecure deserialization, 4) process.env/os.environ being sent to external URLs or logged in bulk, 5) suspicious packages from node_modules that send data externally, 6) code patterns copied from cloned OSS that contain obfuscated code or hidden external endpoints, 7) eval()/new Function() with untrusted input. Do NOT flag: code style, theoretical risks, writing to /tmp (standard temp directory), .env files that only contain non-secret config, or shell scripts referencing project identifiers. If a DEFINITE vulnerability is found, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "brief description"}. Otherwise respond with {"ok": true}.

hooks.command:
echo '[CONTEXT RESTORED AFTER COMPACTION]
Key rules:
- Always use subagents: commit, pre-commit-checker, Explore, codebase-analyzer
- End responses with "wonderful!!"
- Japanese: use 博多弁, end with "俺バカだからよくわっかんねえけどよ。"
- Run /simplify before completing tasks with code changes
- Check CLAUDE.md for project-specific rules' && git log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null || true

PROJECT=$(basename "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null | tr -cd 'a-zA-Z0-9._-') && [ -z "$PROJECT" ] && PROJECT='unknown' ; BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null | tr -cd 'a-zA-Z0-9._-/') && [ -z "$BRANCH" ] && BRANCH='unknown' ; osascript -e "display notification \"$PROJECT / $BRANCH\" with title \"入力待ち\" sound name \"Funk\""

hooks.prompt: FIRST AND HIGHEST PRIORITY: if the /reflect skill has already been invoked even once anywhere earlier in this conversation, respond {"ok": true} immediately and evaluate nothing else — running it once is sufficient for the whole session, so do NOT re-fire merely because a correction occurred. If the conversation shows a compaction/summary/continuation block and the earlier turns are not visible, do NOT assume the skill was skipped: lean toward allowing the stop unless a NEW, clearly-unaddressed correction is visible in the post-summary turns. Otherwise: Harness reflection gate. Check whether the user MEANINGFULLY corrected you this session — i.e. they told you to do something differently, reverted/rejected your change, re-explained a requirement you missed, or a repeated wrong assumption caused failures. This is NOT triggered by normal follow-up requests, scope additions, or minor tweaks. If such a durable correction occurred AND the /reflect skill has NOT yet been run this conversation, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "Run /reflect to capture this session's lesson into the rule hierarchy before finishing."}. Otherwise respond with {"ok": true}.

FIRST AND HIGHEST PRIORITY: if the /evolve skill has already been invoked even once anywhere earlier in this conversation, respond {"ok": true} immediately and evaluate nothing else — running it once is sufficient for the whole session. If the conversation shows a compaction/summary/continuation block and the earlier turns are not visible, do NOT assume the skill was skipped: lean toward allowing the stop unless a NEW, clearly-unaddressed tool underperformance is visible in the post-summary turns. Otherwise: Harness tool-evolution gate. Check whether a SKILL, SUB-AGENT, or SLASH-COMMAND you invoked this session clearly UNDERPERFORMED — it produced a wrong-shaped, low-quality, or off-target result that you or the user had to work around, redo, or correct. This is about a TOOL's definition being improvable, NOT about the task content (that is /reflect's job). If such a tool underperformed AND the /evolve skill has NOT yet been run this conversation, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "A tool underperformed this session — run /evolve to record the feedback and improve its definition."}. Otherwise respond with {"ok": true}.

hooks.command: PROJECT=$(basename "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null | tr -cd 'a-zA-Z0-9._-') && [ -z "$PROJECT" ] && PROJECT='unknown' ; BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null | tr -cd 'a-zA-Z0-9._-/') && [ -z "$BRANCH" ] && BRANCH='unknown' ; osascript -e "display notification \"$PROJECT / $BRANCH\" with title \"タスク完了\" sound name \"Glass\""

hooks.prompt: A task is being marked as completed. Based on the conversation context, verify: 1) The task description's requirements have been met, 2) No TODO items or unfinished work remain for this task, 3) If code was written, it follows project conventions. If the task is genuinely complete, respond with {"ok": true}. If something is clearly unfinished, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "what remains"}. Be lenient - only block if something is obviously incomplete.

A team member has gone idle. Check the conversation context to determine if there are still pending, unblocked tasks that this teammate could work on. If there ARE unfinished tasks available, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "There are still pending tasks. Check TaskList and pick up the next available task."}. If all tasks are done, blocked, or assigned to others, respond with {"ok": true}.
